input.topic: a skilled barista crafting a specialty coffee drink in a modern minimalist cafe

character.name: The Barista

story_arc: Frame 1 (8s): [brew] — Barista pulls an espresso shot while chatting with a customer off-screen.
Frame 2 (7s): [steam] — Barista steams oat milk to silky microfoam.
Frame 3 (7s): [serve] — Barista pours latte art and serves the drink to the customer with a smile.

output.topic: a skilled barista crafting a specialty coffee drink in a modern minimalist cafe
output.full_story: A skilled barista pulls an espresso shot while warmly greeting a customer. She steams oat milk to a silky microfoam and pours an intricate latte art pattern. The sequence concludes as she serves the finished drink with a friendly smile.

frames.prompt: Medium hyperrealistic dolly shot pushing slowly toward a young woman barista in a fitted black apron over a white shirt, standing at a gleaming espresso machine in a modern minimalist cafe. The barista locks a portafilter into the machine and starts the shot, looking up to smile warmly. Barista: (smiling warmly at someone off-camera) "One oat flat white, coming right up!" The espresso begins to flow into a small white ceramic espresso cup. Hyperrealistic style, shallow depth of field, warm tone. The warm Edison lights hang above the counter. The quiet murmur of background cafe atmosphere is continuous to the end of the clip.

Continuing from the espresso pull, the barista pivots and submerges the steam wand into a steel pitcher of oat milk. She holds the pitcher steady as the milk swirls into a fast vortex, tapping it twice on the counter to settle the silky microfoam. The continuous quiet murmur of the cafe is joined by the sustained hiss of the steam wand, filling the soundscape to the final second.

Extending from the steaming, the barista pours the milk over the espresso, forming a clean rosetted leaf. She slides the finished cup across the wooden counter. Barista: (sliding the cup forward cheerfully) "Enjoy your coffee!" The continuous cafe murmur persists as the glass clinks softly against the wood to the end of the clip.
